Good game but it's unplayable on new hardware.

All textures flicker

way better than watchdogs 1 in so many ways! The hacking and the gameplay is so much better. 

buy on a sale and do not pay full price for this though, it's on sale all the time so just wait on that.
one of the best games ever released! extremely underrated and such a good game

Ah man this game is such a disappointment, unlike Aiden pierce the main character is so lame. They characters in this game are just cringy hipsters. The story is completely uninteresting, the game just reeks of lame millennial writing. The series literally went from a depressed fixer who burns who way through the city on a revenge trail, to pseudo communist hipsters. Also the fact the main character starts with a stun gun is so lame.

This game used to be so fun to play. I've played it for over 80 hours on my previous rig and it was a joy. However, ever since I got my new rig, it has become unplayable. Bodies start flying everywhere when taken out, which throws stealth out the window as the bodies alert the guards. Dashing makes me run as if on a grid around walls and places for cover. I can't play like this. I've changed several settings, even lowering all settings to the lowest level and nothing changed. Unfortunately, the only Watch Dogs game I can play at this point is the much inferior Legion game.

If you have a newer PC, don't buy this. You won't have a good time.

I liked this one a lot. It has a completely different energy from the first game — brighter, lighter, more playful, and honestly just more fun to spend time in.

San Francisco is a great open world here. It feels colorful, alive, and full of little places you want to mess around in. The game gives you a phone, drones, cameras, cars, traffic lights, security systems, and basically says, “Go be a problem.” And I respect that kind of trust.

The best part for me is how many ways you can handle missions. You can sneak in, use gadgets, send the jumper, fly the drone, hack half the building from outside, or make one tiny mistake and accidentally turn a clean operation into a tech-themed circus. It still counts if you meant to improvise.

Marcus and DedSec also make the whole thing more enjoyable. The crew has personality, the tone is more relaxed, and the game doesn’t feel like it’s trying to be dark every second. It’s stylish, fun, creative, and one of the better Ubisoft open worlds for just messing around and seeing what happens.

not as good as the first at all. the controls are different and i dont like the story as much, and it is kinda confusing.

its better as a open world multiplayer sandbox like gta online rather than a story game

The absolute peak of Ubisoft’s open-world golden era that turns the San Francisco Bay Area into a glorious hacker playground.

While the original game took itself way too seriously with a grim, brooding protagonist, Watch Dogs 2 is a beautifully vibrant, incredibly fun neon-soaked ride that completely revitalized the franchise. Playing as Marcus Holloway alongside the chaotic, tech-savvy hacker collective DedSec is pure entertainment. Ubisoft masterfully replaced the generic "climb a radio tower to unlock the map" checklist formula with absolute sandbox freedom. The level of player agency here is spectacular: you can remotely hack parked cars to steer them into guards, forge police warrants to have rival gang members arrested on the street, or simply sit on a roof across the street while navigating your RC Jumper and Quadcopter drone through air vents to steal encrypted data.

The true star of the game is its masterclass in open-world environmental design. The condensed recreation of San Francisco, Oakland, and Silicon Valley feels incredibly organic, lively, and reactive. NPCs dynamically interact with one another, call the cops on reckless drivers, or pose for selfies when they see you pulling out your camera. What makes Watch Dogs 2 highly rated on Steam is its immense flexibility in playstyles. If you want to play it as a pure stealth puzzle, the game fully supports a completely non-lethal ghost run using nothing but your remote-control gadgets, hacks, and a stun gun. The driving mechanics are snappy and arcade-focused, the licensed soundtrack is phenomenal, and the seamless multiplayer drop-in events add a brilliant layer of unpredictable chaos.

Ultimately, time has been incredibly kind to this title, with the PC community recognizing it as one of the most creative, unrestrictive, and genuinely joyful open-world sandboxes ever coded. It completely sheds the corporate fatigue of later entries in the series, leaning heavily into a fun-loving, colorful, and highly stylized tech-rebellion vibe. It runs beautifully on modern hardware and scales perfectly to high refresh rates, making it an absolute "no-brainer" recommendation for anyone looking for a rich sandbox where creativity is heavily rewarded. It is an absolute classic that stands tall as a pinnacle of open-world design.

9/10 — I cleared an entire heavily-guarded FBI fortress without ever stepping foot inside the building, purely by controlling a tiny toy car and hacking a security camera. Peak gaming freedom.
this game is actually goood! just need more activities around the city! but overall great game!!
Very fun, but you have to do some workarounds on newer Nvidia GPUs to avoid flickering issues. One of the workarounds disables online play, which removes some fun aspects of the game.
Story is not that good but gameplay overall is much better than Watch Dogs 1. City in this one is a very fun playground and has a more brighter vibe than 1.

Open world + hacker + stealth. Very fun but not many people like the play style.....but I like it. Need to create ubisoft acc but after that can login offline. There are helicopter but cant be use, can hack building crane instead.

Watch Dogs 2, arguably best in the 3-logy, most fun, best characters ( aside from Aiden ). I have already played the game, 2 times in the past but I said to myself - lets walkthrough the story the 3x time. 

And I wasnt bored, little easy even on the highest difficulty, but still the hacking, parkour, even action, puzzles, were enjoying.

The story, yes it isnt really deep. If you are really trying to understand what WD2 is all about, then just enjoy the gameplay and f-up everything you see in your sight. 

When you had a tough day, this games is good to chillout to if you dont have anything else to do. 

Defo recommened to everybody. You can skip the first game as there is really no continuity in the story aspect of the franchise, but if you want to understand the lore and the world enjoy everything Ubisoft made for WD ( apart from the Legion - the Legion Aiden, Wrench DLC)

9/10 Ubisoft, there was a time you made good games ( otherwise would be 10/10)
I forgot how fun this game was! it's nice to comeback to Watch Dogs 2 to have fun and remember the days when the name "Ubisoft" was associated with a game, it usually meant you would enjoy that game.
This game is very much enjoyable 10yrs later. You can expect 40-50FPs. It looks great even docked. You can play at medium or high settings.

Finally had the time to finish the game and I would say that most of the game mechanics are fun to play and tinker around with. The game also provides multiple ways for you to actually finish a mission. The only downside is the story portion of the game which is at most times badly written and sometimes feel like the characters are just kids that have hacking abilities. The dialogues are that cringe. Still a decent game in my opinion.
This game is the best out of the whole Watch Dogs IP despite not having the customization of Legion. Everything feels very refined and looks great!

Got softlocked because cable phisics are trash and cranes go crazier the more time you play in a single session, the sky flickers, bodies fly when killed, random crashes when angering a rival band, your character randomly forgets how to steal cars, elevating the drone sometimes equals driving forward if you're in a car, the first game had issues, but this game hasn't had a QA phase at all. The story can be summarized in a mix between a cheap "we are the edgy digital revels against the big brother" propaganda and "wah wah, we're not script kiddies, we are hackers, our ego is bigger than Saturn, you don't know who you're playing with", for crying out loud, some of the conversations feel like were written by 14yos. Some missions are just lame cinematics, the first game revolves around vengance for the death of ..., here a main character dies and it's like "oh no, nevermind, destroy some drugs, casually kill the killers and all solved, also, this is just a simple distraction, we still need to show Blume we have the bigger ♥♥♥♥"
great game used to play at a ton when i was a teen on my ps4 back in 2016 just got it again on steam and reliving childhood memories

At first feel a bit weird play play part 2 of Watch Dogs franchise cause it slighly different than the first part the whole vibe is different from a man with family to a young group. World Building mix with Hacking is more realistic and more way to hack than just explosive, a bit harder than the first part and not just normal parkour to move around building and can hack more stuff to move around or distraction.
Ton of bugs. Main characters pretend to be cool but just freaks, But gameplay is fun. GTA 5 on lowest settings.

As an open world game, this title still holds up very well; furthermore, based on the portion of the story I’ve played so far, it is good. To me, the most fascinating aspect of this game is the hacking. The game offers extensive opportunities for hacking and gadget usage, which makes playing it incredibly enjoyable...

I played the first game in 2018 and I loved the game for how dark and serious Aiden Pearce was after what happened to his family and I loved playing Watch_Dogs 2 in 2018 with the hacking group DedSec as they cause chaos in San Francisco. I played the idea of creating weapons and gadgets with the 3D printer while driving cool vehicles and flying the drone. I loved the idea of fighting against Blume for their malicious ideas and tactics to control everything and I loved that Ubisoft predicted the future with AI taking over the world and tech companies like Blume collecting data from every household. I like the customization of Marcus, his vehicles, his weapons and the DecSec radio music. I love this game for how fun the missions are and how the game foreshadow what would happen only 5 years later. I find it interesting that the first game was very serious like GTA 4 and Watch_Dogs 2 being more relaxed and colorful like GTA 5, I enjoyed both games for how accurate they predicted life in the 2020s and I recommend this game to anyone who enjoyed Saints Row, Cyberpunk 2077, GTA 5, Dying Light.

If you want a game that feels like GTA, do NOT play this.
After completing the story mode in GTA 5, i wanted to look for another GTA like game. I came across this game and it felt interesting.
I've completed 39% of this game and it has been an awful experience.
The puzzles are hard in some missions, which i have to restort to YouTube videos just to get past them.
And that "Jabberton Hacker Challange" or whatever it's called, good luck with that. That has to be the worst part of this game. While the team is trying to help you, the only thing they do is move a piece over and over again until something happens.
If that's not the only painful part of the game, wait until you get to defusing Lenni's trap, where you have 5 minutes to do the same thing as in the hacker challange, but the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ parttern gets scrambled every time you solve one side.
In GTA 5, If you fail the mission 3 times, you get a button to skip the section. In this game you're expected to finish the mission without dying, with no skips whatsoever.
Even GTA is easier to beat than this mess of a game.
3/10, would not reccomend.
Set in the vibrant San Francisco, Watch Dogs 2 offers a GTA-like gameplay with edgy characters and the ability to hack basically anything

Although it was alright in gameplay, it lacked a proper story. I feel like Ubisoft tried too hard to get away from the original watchdogs dark theme. Game is kind of cringe if you know about tech stuff but meh it was alright.

game runs like crap on budget hardware despite the fact its a decade old game. its also just boring. WD1 was 10x better because you felt attached to Aiden. in this game you dont care about the characters and its just too happy. you dont feel like a hacker taking down an evil surveillance system, you just feel like some ♥♥♥♥♥ with makeshift guns doing who knows what. like whats with the whole social media/membership thing? dedsec is supposed to be secret and mysterious but nah in this game you have to get people to become members or something. its nonsense. and in terms of hacking, it feels so bad in WD2. in WD1 it was simple but fun and challenging enough to be engaging. in this game its a burden. the story isnt that bad though, just everything outside of the main story. as soon as you step outside of a main mission, it feels like a GTA V or even GTA VI from what we've seen of it so far. most characters are kinda shallow and their designs are also not what a bunch of computer nerds would look like. the main character is the only one who looks kinda like a hacker. driving also feels horrible which is important to me because i like just hopping on WD1 and driving around if im bored. overall, this is just another example of Ubisoft screwing up sequels. If this game had the art direction, character designs and overall atmosphere/feel of WD1 with some improved visuals (which are hard to beat tbh - the graphics of WD1 are insanely good and well optimised), and also some new, interesting and/or improved features, this would be a great game. but they just had to ruin it. classic Ubisoft. but hey at least its not as bad as Legion, right?

One of my favorite open world games. A huge part of what made me love this game was the incredible rendition of San Francisco as well as a story that is very telling and predictive of our technocratic times.

Watch_Dogs 2 is the last game made by Ubisoft that I would consider great. They actually put effort into this game and I really enjoyed it. San Francisco really felt alive and the NPC interactions felt realistic and the AI was good. The gameplay felt smooth and I really love the hacking. The only thing I could maybe complain about is that the missions felt repetitve because 80% of the time you do the same thing: Infiltrate an enemies base and download something. Classic Ubisoft. Other than that, the game was a great experience for me and I will try to get all achievements.

[h1]Rating: 4/10 -Disappointing[/h1]

[h1]TLDR:[/h1]
[b]A bland, boring and unthriling Watch Dogs game that barely improves upon its predecessor.[/b]


Watch Dogs 1 has impressed me. I ignored it for years thinking: "it's just a GTA clone with hacking". Turns out, it's a flawed gem that has left an impression on me. The dark gritty vibe of Chicago. Aiden Pearce, the vigilante that tries to find the murderer of his niece. ctOS, everything is connected, they listen, big brother is watching you. The game is a thriller, and a good one at that. I was curious of what WD2 will do next. I was sadly disappointed by the whole game. It misses the mark on almost all occasions. It improves a few flaws of the first game but fails tremendously in parts were WD1 was strong.


[h1]Positive:[/h1]

[b]Parkouring Animations:[/b]
WD1 already had parkouring animations. WD2 added a bunch more with style.

[b]Clothes:[/b]
while you were limited to aura farming trench coats in WD1, you now have the freedom to fully customize your outfit. You can change your shoes, pants, jacket, glasses and hat independently. You can equip sets or mix and match whatever you like. The game has different clothing shops for different styles of clothes similar to GTA. Some Silicon Valley company buildings have a gift shop where you're able to buy a T-shirt with their logo on it. I love this detail.

[b]Music:[/b]
WD1 mostly had generic music with a few bangers. WD2 has phenomenal music from start to end. Seriously. The tracks really fit the hacker vibe. The only downside about the music is the fact that if you die in a mission once, the music won't come back. Only awkward silence.

[b]Hackable objects:[/b]
They've vastly improved and increased the amount of hackable objects. You can now hack and move cars. You can control cranes to gain access to rooftops. You can control a remote controlled ground drone and quadcopter to enter and hack buildings remotely. WD1 should have shipped with this amount of hackable objects.


[h1]Negative:[/h1]

[b]Protagonist:[/b]
I've heard people say that Aiden Pearce  from WD1 is lame or has no depth. I disagree. I think, this applies more to Marcus Holloway. He is just as deep as the side characters. He has no aura and talks like a Reddit basement dweller (just like everyone else).

[b]Side Characters/Deadsec:[/b]
They are far too goofy. The situation with Blume and ctOS 2.0 is very serious and they start to realize how much control Blume actually has. Still, they keep on acting like little kids. They are all cornballs. I could not connect to a single character. Deadsec? You mean, Fagsec. Even the main villain is weak as hell. It's not even a villain. Just the CEO of Blume that is just in the background, doing yoga and making threats.

[b]Weapons:[/b]
The game has quite a few weapons to buy. The problem is that just a few are actually good. You start out with a taser and and a pistol. I then bought a silenced rifle called "Zero Day" and played with it through the game. I didn't feel the need to buy anything else. I bought a sniper later on to get some range but that was it. 30+ guns are for nothing really. The rifles feel the same.

[b]Reinforcements:[/b]
Nearly every enemy can call reinforcements now which is annoying. Combat encounters are filled to the brim with callers. One mistake and a ton of enemies will spawn. WD1 only gratualy added callers to encounters. WD2 had them from the beginning of the game.

[b]Shops:[/b]
Driving and parking in front of a shop scares the shop owner and his customers. He gets scared by your driving and he leaves his counter. This causes you to be unable to use the shop. This happens all the time. I have to use fast travel to teleport to a shop to use it properly. This ruins the immersion. I want to be clear. I'm not crashing into the shop. I'm simply driving and stopping in front of the door.

[b]Restaurants:[/b]
They serve no purpose anymore. In Watch Dogs 1 you could go to restaurants and bars to drink something. This would replenish and enhance your focus meter for a short period of time. The focus meter lets you slow down time during gun fights similar to Michael from Grand Theft Auto 5. The focus mechanic has been removed from WD2. You can still visit restaurants and drink something but you don't have to pay and you don't get any benefits from it.

[b]AMD Driver Timouts:[/b]
This game caused 4 driver timeouts which crash the game. It doesn't happen often but the timeouts always occured when I started shooting at an ememy for some reason. I’ve never played a game that caused a driver timeout and I haven't updated my drivers recently.


[h1]Conclusion:[/h1]

An incredibly weak successor to Watch Dogs 1. It improved the weak parts of WD1 like the hackable objects and music but it fails in every other area where WD1 excelled at. A story, that seemed serious at first, is extremely downplayed by a dumb cast of characters. Characters that are as deep as a cardboard cutout. The characters have nothing to say and have zero development. Hard truths are being discovered about the extreme surveillance of Blume but no one feels distressed or anything. The game was over and I felt nothing. The only thing that I'll take with me from this game is a music producer I've discovered called Aphex Twin. I heard one of his tracks in the ingame radio and I really liked it. I won't touch WD Legion obviously. The game looks horrendous. The Watch Dogs franchise is cooked.

im glad there is queer representation in this game as it can satisfy both sides, one can feel proud of being represented in such a good game, and the other one would just drive through the parades like butter

I played Watch Dogs 2 years ago on Xbox, but coming back to it on PC reminded me why this game hits so hard. It’s one of the rare open‑world games that actually rewards you for being creative instead of forcing you into one playstyle. You can go loud, go stealth, or send in drones and RC cars like a gremlin who refuses to touch the ground. All three work, and all three are fun.

Marcus and the DedSec crew still hold up — they’re actually likable, the writing has personality, and the whole San Francisco map feels alive in a way most open‑world games don’t bother with anymore. Even knowing the story beats already, I still found myself messing with NPCs, hacking traffic lights just to watch chaos unfold, and turning forklifts into weapons of mass inconvenience.

Performance on PC is solid, the missions stay fresh, and the hacking tools make you feel like a menace with a smartphone. It’s stylish, it’s fun, and it gives you a ridiculous amount of freedom to solve problems however you want.

If you like stealth, sandbox problem‑solving, or just causing trouble with zero consequences, Watch Dogs 2 is absolutely worth playing — even on a second run.

9/10 — Still one of the best “do whatever you want” sandboxes out there. 10/10 would hack a man’s car and send him into a wall again.
even with a gtx 1650. it can run pretty smoothly with high graphics. i like how you can blow up an orphanage

Super goed en tof spel, zit ook aardig wat hond in. Ook al zijn die beesten soms hinderlijk, maar niet altijd.

Jumping into Watch Dogs 2 is a massive breath of fresh air that completely revitalizes the hacker fantasy without any spoilers. Exploring the incredibly vibrant San Francisco map provides a brilliant tech playground where you can creatively hack everything—from cars to city infrastructure—and swing your trusty thunderball melee weapon to complete missions exactly how you want. The parkour is fluid, and the RC gadgets give you amazing tactical stealth options that never get old. On the downside, the lighthearted, meme-heavy tone of your DedSec crew can feel completely jarring if you suddenly switch to aggressively gunning down security guards during gameplay. As a quick May 2026 internet check for the community, it is a confirmed truth that the live-action Watch Dogs movie starring Tom Blyth has wrapped filming and is leaning heavily into the real-world dangers of modern surveillance. If you want a fun, highly interactive open-world sandbox with endless creative freedom, this is an absolute blast that easily outshines the original. Score 
8.5/10
